# README Builder

> Visual drag-and-drop editor for GitHub READMEs

## What it does

README Builder is a free, open-source web application that lets developers build GitHub-flavored READMEs using a visual block-based editor instead of writing raw markdown. Users drag blocks (headings, badges, code snippets, tables, images, alerts, etc.) into place, see a live GitHub-style preview, and export the result as a `.md` file.

## Key features

- 15+ block types: headings, badges, code, tables, lists, images, alerts, details/spoilers, blockquotes, columns, social links, GitShow card
- Live GitHub-flavored preview with dark/light mode toggle
- Drag-and-drop block reordering
- Visual badge editor with shields.io integration
- Import from raw markdown, local files, or any GitHub repository URL
- 4 starter templates: Minimal Library, Full Project, CLI Tool, GitHub Profile
- SEO-optimized template output with proper heading hierarchy and alt text
- Undo/redo with full history
- Auto-save to localStorage
- Markdown source tab with CodeMirror syntax highlighting
- Fully client-side, no backend, no auth, no tracking

## Links

- Live app: https://ofershap.github.io/readme-builder/
- GitHub: https://github.com/ofershap/readme-builder
- License: MIT

## Tech stack

React 19, TypeScript (strict), Tailwind CSS 4, Vite 7, Zustand + zundo, CodeMirror 6, @dnd-kit/react, react-markdown + remark-gfm + rehype-raw

## Usage

Open https://ofershap.github.io/readme-builder/ in any browser. No installation or account needed. Build your README visually, then click "Copy" or "Export" to get the markdown.

## Self-hosting

```
git clone https://github.com/ofershap/readme-builder.git
cd readme-builder
npm install
npm run dev
```
